ddls:
CREATE TABLE "directories" (
  "directory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each directory */,
  "directory_name" TEXT /* Name of the directory */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the directory */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the directory was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the directory was last updated */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the directory belongs to */,
  "last_accessed_at" TEXT /* Date the directory was last accessed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the directory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("directory_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assemblies" (
  "assembly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assembly */,
  "directory_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the directory where the assembly is located */,
  "assembly_name" TEXT /* Name of the assembly */,
  "assembly_version" TEXT /* Version of the assembly */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the assembly was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the assembly was last updated */,
  "assembly_type" TEXT /* Type of the assembly */,
  "license" TEXT /* License information of the assembly */,
  "last_checked_at" TEXT /* Date the assembly was last checked for updates */,
  "is_outdated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the assembly is outdated */,
  "security_rating" TEXT /* Security rating of the assembly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assembly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assemblies_directory_id FOREIGN KEY ("directory_id") REFERENCES directories ("directory_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "referencing_assemblies" (
  "ref_assembly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each referencing assembly */,
  "assembly_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the assembly that is being referenced */,
  "referencing_assembly_name" TEXT /* Name of the assembly that is referencing the assembly */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the reference was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the reference was last updated */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the referencing assembly is critical */,
  "last_checked_at" TEXT /* Date the reference was last checked for issues */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ref_assembly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_referencing_assemblies_assembly_id FOREIGN KEY ("assembly_id") REFERENCES assemblies ("assembly_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "issues" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each issue */,
  "assembly_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the assembly that has the issue */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the issue */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the type of problem */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the issue */,
  "remediation" TEXT /* Steps to remediate the issue */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the issue was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the issue was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the issue */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the issue belongs to */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the issue */,
  "assigned_to_user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user assigned to the issue */,
  "resolved_by_user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who resolved the issue */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date the issue was resolved */,
  "reopened_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the issue was reopened */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_resolved_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES directories ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_assigned_to_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_assembly_id FOREIGN KEY ("assembly_id") REFERENCES assemblies ("assembly_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_active_at" TEXT /* Date the user was last active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "issue_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* ID of the issue being acted upon */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was performed */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the issue belongs to */,
  "is_automated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the action was automated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issue_logs_issue_id FOREIGN KEY ("issue_id") REFERENCES issues ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issue_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issue_logs_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES directories ("project_id")
)
